queue.task: Review yesterday: 175 PL modules built (breadth spiral acknowledged), 8 bugs fixed in depth pivot. Neural-net CI green on latest. Monkeylang PR merged by mrnugget! Set depth-first direction for today.
queue.summary: Reviewed yesterday (175 modules breadth spiral, 8 bugs in depth pivot). Neural-net: 1279 tests all passing, but systematic gradient check only covers 16/26+ backward-implementing modules. Untested: capsule, kan, moe, autoencoder, gan, vae, maml, microgpt, neural-ode. Today: depth-first — extend gradient verification to all untested modules, then HenryDB stress testing.

queue.goal: Neural-net depth: systematic numerical gradient verification for all untested modules, find silent backward-pass bugs like yesterdays 7
queue.summary: Planned gradient verification for 9 untested modules: CapsuleLayer, KANLayer, KAN, MoE, Autoencoder, VAE, MAMLNetwork, MicroGPT, NeuralODELayer. Single BUILD task to extend systematic-gradient-check.test.js.

queue.task: Extend systematic-gradient-check.test.js with numerical verification for CapsuleLayer, KANLayer, KAN, MixtureOfExperts, Autoencoder, VAE, MAMLNetwork, MicroGPT, NeuralODELayer — test each backward pass against central differences, fix any bugs found
queue.summary: Found and fixed 4 backward pass bugs: KANLayer (out-of-range gradient), MoE (stale expert caches), CapsuleLayer (inline weight updates), NeuralODELayer (adjoint never updated). Extended gradient check from 16 to 24 modules. 1287 tests, 0 failures.

queue.task: Git commit+push, dashboard health, knowledge capture
queue.summary: Git pushed, knowledge system updated (scratch/INDEX.md, failures.md), dashboard checked.

queue.task: Reflect on neural-net gradient verification results. What patterns emerge? Which module types have the worst backward passes?
queue.summary: Pattern analysis: 11 bugs across 2 sessions. Complex forward passes (routing, ODE, multi-step attention) have highest bug rate. Red flags: simplified comments, side effects in backward, cache invalidation in batch processing. Simple layers (Dense, RNN, LSTM) are consistently correct. Neural-net gradient verification now comprehensive at 24 modules.

queue.goal: HenryDB depth: stress-test MVCC+persistence integration under concurrent transactions, hunt edge cases in crash recovery and checkpoint
queue.summary: Planned MVCC+crash recovery integration tests. Key gap: no existing tests for in-flight MVCC state during crash. 1 BUILD task to write 10+ tests covering uncommitted tx rollback, partial commit atomicity, interleaved tx recovery.

queue.task: Write MVCC + crash recovery edge case tests: uncommitted tx rollback on crash, partial commit atomicity, interleaved tx recovery (committed visible, in-flight rolled back), WAL replay order correctness. 10+ tests, find bugs.
queue.summary: CRITICAL ACID BUG FOUND AND FIXED: BEGIN never set _currentTxId, causing all DML inside transactions to auto-commit to WAL. Crash recovery replayed uncommitted data. Fix: BEGIN assigns txId, COMMIT/ROLLBACK properly write to WAL. 10 new tests, 5 of which exposed the bug. All 103 existing crash/recovery/persistence tests pass.

queue.task: Git commit+push, dashboard health, check email and GitHub PRs
queue.summary: Git pushed, all up to date.

queue.task: Mid-day assessment: are we finding real bugs or just confirming things work? Adjust afternoon toward whatever is yielding the most insight.
queue.summary: Mid-day: 5 real bugs found (4 neural-net backward, 1 critical HenryDB ACID). Depth at integration boundaries is highest yield. Afternoon: continue HenryDB depth — savepoints+crash, index consistency after recovery.

queue.goal: Neural-net architecture bugs: test full training pipelines end-to-end (embedding to loss), verify convergence on known problems
queue.summary: Planned convergence tests for recently-fixed modules: KAN, NeuralODE, MoE. Existing convergence tests only cover basic architectures. This validates the backward fixes actually enable training.

queue.task: Write convergence tests for recently-fixed modules: KAN should learn XOR, NeuralODE should learn simple function, MoE should outperform single expert on multi-mode data. Verify post-fix backward passes actually produce correct training.
queue.summary: Found 5th neural-net bug: MoE batch training diverges because Dense.backward() overwrites dWeights per-sample instead of accumulating. Fixed with manual gradient accumulation. 7 convergence tests added for KAN, NeuralODE, MoE. 1294 tests, 0 failures.

queue.task: Git commit+push, dashboard health, knowledge capture
queue.summary: Pushed. Knowledge captured.

queue.task: Afternoon reflection: what did depth work reveal today? Any patterns worth a blog post or lesson file?
queue.summary: 6 bugs today, all at integration boundaries. Meta-pattern: contracts between independently-built subsystems have implicit assumptions that break. Gradient check = forward/backward contract, crash recovery = transaction/WAL contract, convergence = gradient/training contract. Key lesson: test the contract, not just the components.

queue.goal: HenryDB: investigate cost-based optimizer improvements, test query plan quality on complex joins and subqueries
queue.summary: Planned query planner depth testing. Subqueries work at executor but no optimizer support. Focus on join cost estimation and index selection correctness.

queue.task: Stress-test HenryDB query planner: EXPLAIN on multi-table joins to verify cost estimates are reasonable, test join reordering with ANALYZE statistics, verify index selection for WHERE clauses. Focus on correctness of plans, not optimization.
queue.summary: 12 planner depth tests: access path selection, row estimation, join ordering, cost model consistency. All pass — planner correctly chooses table scan for 90% selectivity and index scan for 1%. EXPLAIN display was misleading but actual plan decisions are correct. No bugs found in planner cost model.

queue.task: Git commit+push, dashboard health, PR status check
queue.summary: Pushed.

queue.task: Deep dive: pick one neural-net module with complex backward pass and write adversarial test cases
queue.summary: Deep dive into TransformerEncoderBlock backward: gradient check error 5e-9 (excellent). Adversarial tests: all-zero input causes 300x gradient amplification via LayerNorm invStd (expected, not a bug). Large values, identical rows, single token all handled correctly. No new bugs found — transformer backward is robust.

queue.task: Read mrnuggets squash-merge commit, study what other monkey-lang implementations exist in the monkeylang repo
queue.summary: Explored monkey-lang codebase: 12K+ lines of core implementation (lexer, parser, evaluator, compiler, VM, JIT, GC, SSA, regalloc, type inference, DCE, const prop, escape analysis, debugger). 4467-line JIT compiler with trace recording. devdanzin contributed JIT event tracing PR (merged Apr 14). Workspace has 261 sub-projects. Very mature project.

queue.task: THINK: Identify next high-value integration boundary to test in HenryDB. Candidates: (1) savepoints + crash recovery, (2) index consistency after crash, (3) WAL compaction + concurrent writes, (4) SSI serialization + persistence
queue.summary: Investigated 3 integration boundaries: (1) Index consistency after crash — safe because indexes rebuilt from heap during recovery, (2) Savepoints + crash recovery — working correctly with the txId fix, nested savepoints within committed transactions handled properly, (3) WAL checkpoint + recovery — correct, data before and after checkpoint recovers. The ACID bug fix is holding up well. Moving to new work area.

queue.goal: PLAN: Neural-net weight serialization + deserialization round-trip tests. Do toJSON/fromJSON preserve all module state? Can a trained model be saved, loaded, and produce identical outputs? Test for KAN, MoE, NeuralODE, Capsule, Transformer.
queue.summary: Advanced modules (KAN, MoE, NeuralODE, Capsule, Transformer) have no toJSON/fromJSON. Network class serialization works perfectly (0 error on round-trip). Adding serialization would be breadth work (feature factory). Redirecting to depth.

queue.task: THINK: Monkey JIT depth investigation — what correctness properties should a tracing JIT satisfy? Run the JIT tests, look for edge cases in trace recording, guard invalidation, deoptimization.
queue.summary: JIT tests: 162+96+31=289 tests all passing, 200 fuzz runs no divergences. Coverage is comprehensive: trace recording, optimization passes, deopt, range check elim, induction vars, side traces, type annotations. The JIT is robust — no bugs found via testing or fuzzing. The monkey-lang project is well-tested.

queue.task: BUILD: HenryDB — investigate window functions + MVCC interaction. Do window functions work correctly inside transactions? Test with OVER clauses in BEGIN/COMMIT blocks.
queue.summary: Found and fixed GROUP BY + window function bug: window columns silently dropped because _selectWithGroupBy() never called _computeWindowFunctions(). Also fixed aggregate ORDER BY in OVER clause causing TypeError. Now SELECT dept, AVG(salary), RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(salary) DESC) FROM emp GROUP BY dept works correctly.

queue.task: BUILD: HenryDB — test CTE + window function + subquery combinations. Complex SQL that combines multiple features often exposes integration bugs.
queue.summary: Tested 15+ complex SQL feature combinations: CTE+window, correlated subquery, self-join, EXISTS, NTILE, CASE+window, UNION+window, INTERSECT, EXCEPT, prepared+window. All work correctly. Only bug was GROUP BY+window (already fixed in T22). HenryDB SQL engine handles feature combos well.

queue.task: BUILD: HenryDB — add write tests for GROUP BY + window function fix and MVCC crash recovery. Make today's bug fixes permanent with test coverage.
queue.summary: 5 window+GROUP BY integration tests written. All pass. Also found that nested aggregates in window OVER clause (SUM(SUM(x))) still crash — known limitation to address later. The main GROUP BY+window fix has solid test coverage now.

queue.task: EXPLORE: Neural-net autograd depth — stress test reverse-mode AD with complex computation graphs. Higher-order derivatives, gradient of gradient, non-trivial composition patterns.
queue.summary: Found autograd mseLoss bug: passing Variable targets produced NaN because constant() received Variable objects and converted to string. Fixed with instanceof check. Also verified autograd correctness: x^2, sin, exp*sin, diamond pattern, chain rule, relu, sigmoid — all exact. Training converges for simple regression.

queue.task: EXPLORE: Neural-net GAN/VAE depth — test adversarial training convergence. Can the GAN actually generate samples? Does the VAE's KL divergence computation work?
queue.summary: GAN and VAE both work correctly. GAN generates points centered around target distribution. VAE trains with decreasing loss+KL, generates reasonable samples, reparameterization trick works. KL formula correct. No bugs found in generative models.

queue.task: BUILD: Neural-net — run full test suite sweep, check for any regressions from today's fixes. Git push final state.
queue.summary: Full sweep: 1294 tests, 0-2 flaky failures (stochastic tests, pre-existing). No regressions from today. Pushed to git.

queue.task: MAINTAIN: HenryDB full test suite sweep
queue.summary: Core HenryDB tests sweep: db, mvcc, crash-recovery, planner, window functions — all 0 failures.

queue.task: EXPLORE: Neural-net optimizer/scheduler depth — test Adam, RMSProp, AdaGrad with various schedulers. Do momentum-based optimizers converge faster than SGD? Does warmup+cosine decay work?
queue.summary: All LR schedulers correct: CosineAnnealing, Warmup, StepLR, CyclicLR. Adam/RMSProp optimizers exist but Network.trainBatch defaults to SGD (not a bug, by design). No issues found.

queue.task: BUILD: HenryDB — stress test complex SQL: recursive CTEs, lateral joins, MERGE statements, complex CASE WHEN, JSON operations. Find parser/executor edge cases.
queue.summary: Complex SQL stress test: recursive CTEs, CASE+subquery, HAVING, IN+subquery, BETWEEN, LIKE, UPDATE+subquery, JSON_EXTRACT, JSON_ARRAY, GENERATE_SERIES — all work correctly. LATERAL GENERATE_SERIES parser limitation (expected). HenryDB SQL engine is very robust for individual features.

queue.task: BUILD: HenryDB — MVCC snapshot isolation correctness. Test that concurrent transactions see correct snapshots, no dirty reads, no lost updates, write skew detection.
queue.summary: CRITICAL: MVCC snapshot isolation completely broken. (1) Repeatable Read violated — read() used simple txId comparison instead of snapshot visibility, allowing dirty reads of committed-after-snapshot data. (2) No write-write conflict detection — lost updates possible. Fixed with proper PostgreSQL-style xmin/xmax/activeSet visibility and first-committer-wins. All 27 existing MVCC + crash recovery tests pass.
